# Axion Mobile 1.0.0

A React Native (Expo) port of the [Axion desktop](https://github.com/almostkoi/axion)
music player, built for phones. Offline-first, scans the device's audio
library, plays in the background with full lockscreen / notification controls.

> **Status:** beta — first public release.
> Companion to [Axion desktop](https://github.com/almostkoi/axion/releases/tag/v1.0.0).

---

## Highlights

- **Offline-first** — scans the device's audio library via the Android media store; no internet required for playback.
- **Background playback** with full lockscreen / notification controls, headset and Bluetooth media keys (powered by `react-native-track-player`).
- **Native MediaSession** integration — Wear OS, Android Auto stub, car-stereo and earbud controls just work.
- **UI** — dark theme matching the desktop app: Home, Library (Songs / Albums / Artists / Playlists), Search, Settings, Now Playing, Queue.
- **Persistent mini-player** above the tab bar; tap to expand into the full Now Playing view.
- **Library schema mirrors the desktop app**, so playlists and play counts can be ported across.
- **YouTube import** routed through Piped → Invidious → direct extraction, with live instance discovery.
- **Haptic feedback** on long-press, swipe and key actions.

## Downloads

Most modern phones (anything from 2019 onwards) want **`arm64-v8a`**. If you're
not sure, grab the universal APK — it works everywhere but is bigger.

| File | Size | Recommended for |
|---|---|---|
| `Axion-1.0.0-arm64-v8a.apk` | ~31 MB | **Most users.** All modern 64-bit Android phones. |
| `Axion-1.0.0-armeabi-v7a.apk` | ~25 MB | Older / cheaper 32-bit devices (Android 8–10 era). |
| `Axion-1.0.0-x86_64.apk` | ~33 MB | Emulators and Chromebooks. |
| `Axion-1.0.0-universal.apk` | ~83 MB | "I don't know which one" — a fat APK that contains every architecture. |

> **Google Play / F-Droid:** not yet — sideload via the APKs above for the
> first release.

## Installation (Android sideload)

1. Download one of the APKs above to your phone (browser, USB transfer, or cloud).
2. Open it. Android will ask whether to allow installs from this source —
   accept (you can revoke afterwards in **Settings → Apps → Special access →
   Install unknown apps**).
3. Tap **Install**.
4. Launch **Axion**. Grant the **Music & audio** permission so it can read your library.
5. The library scan starts automatically; tracks appear as they're parsed.

> **First-launch tip:** if no music shows up, make sure you granted the
> **Music & audio** (Android 13+) or **Storage** (Android 12 and below)
> permission. Toggle it in **Settings → Apps → Axion → Permissions**.

## Permissions explained

| Permission | Why |
|---|---|
| `READ_MEDIA_AUDIO` (Android 13+) / `READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE` | Scan your music library — Axion never uploads it anywhere. |
| `FOREGROUND_SERVICE` + `FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PLAYBACK` | Keep playback running with the screen off / app backgrounded. |
| `WAKE_LOCK` | Prevent the OS from killing the audio service mid-track. |
| `INTERNET` | **Only** used when you trigger a YouTube import. No analytics, no telemetry. |

## What's new

Initial public release.

## Known limitations

- **iOS not yet released** — all React Native code is iOS-compatible, but no signed IPA is shipped for v1.0.
- **YouTube imports** rely on public Piped and Invidious instances and may fail when those are rate-limited or down. If everything's dead, use the [desktop app](https://github.com/almostkoi/axion) to import and sync the resulting files to your phone.
- **No Android Auto / Wear OS UI** — only basic MediaSession integration. Native automotive UI is a v1.x goal.
- **First scan** of a large library (>10 000 tracks) can take 30–60 s on older devices. Subsequent launches reuse the cached database.

## System requirements

- **Android 8.0 (Oreo, API 26)** or newer
- ~100 MB free storage for the app + ~50 MB more for the cached library database
- Plus the size of your music library

## Verifying the APK (optional)

```powershell
# Windows
Get-FileHash .\Axion-1.0.0-arm64-v8a.apk -Algorithm SHA256
```

```bash
# macOS / Linux
sha256sum Axion-1.0.0-arm64-v8a.apk
```

The APK is signed with this certificate:

```
Owner:   CN=almostkoi, OU=Axion, O=Axion
Issuer:  same (self-signed)
Validity: until 2056
```

You can verify the signature on a Linux/macOS box with `apksigner`:

```bash
apksigner verify --verbose Axion-1.0.0-arm64-v8a.apk
```

## Reporting issues

Open an issue with:

- Axion Mobile version (Settings → About)
- Phone model + Android version
- Steps to reproduce
- Logs via `adb logcat -s AxionMobile:V ReactNativeJS:V *:S` if you can capture them
